statement.markdown: Implement a function to print values from a singly linked list in a forward manner. The function should start printing from the head of the list.
statement.inputFormat: Input a singly linked list with values separated by spaces
statement.outputFormat: A string of values in the linked list separated by spaces
statement.notes: Use a StringBuilder for efficiency if the language supports it.

constraints.description: Number of nodes in the list

examples.explanation: Print values from head to tail

hints.text: Consider a StringBuilder-like approach to store node values and return as a string.

editorial.title: Fixing the LinkedList Print Function
editorial.intuition: The problem asks us to create a function to print values in a singly linked list starting from the head.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute force approach is to traverse the linked list using recursion.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a StringBuilder-like approach to store node values efficiently.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal approach is to traverse the linked list iteratively while storing node values in a StringBuilder.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correct solution prints values from head to tail of the linked list.
editorial.dryRun: Print values from a linked list: [1, 2, 3] and [4, 5, 6].
commonMistakes: forgetting to print node values in the loop
